note: SAE J1772_202401 exists on sae.org (paywalled but domain/standard confirmed via search). Standard exists and is current (2024 revision). NEVI CCS1 requirement per 23 CFR Part 680 confirmed. NACS J3400 published Dec 2023 (TIR) and Sept 2024 (Recommended Practice) — timeline in row aligns. mandatory_status 'De facto mandatory' is appropriate. Gap description accurate.

Source URL was 'sae.org/standards/content/j3400_202309/' — version suffix _202309 is wrong; first issue was J3400_202312 (December 2023). URL has been corrected to 'sae.org/standards/content/j3400/' (canonical landing page). Standards and gap content are accurate: CCS1 federally required at NEVI stations per 23 CFR Part 680; NACS adopted by major OEMs 2024-2025; GB/T 20234.3 physically incompatible. FHWA NEVI update allowing NACS adapters confirmed (May 2023 guidance, not a full 2024 rule change as implied — verify exact regulatory status of NACS as replacement vs. supplement).

Source URL 'fcc.gov/engineering-technology/laboratory-division/general/equipment-authorization/about-equipment-authorization' returned 404. Corrected to 'fcc.gov/engineering-technology/laboratory-division/general/equipment-authorization' (confirmed live). FCC Part 15 Subpart B SDoC requirement for Class B digital devices is accurate. Mandatory status correct. Gap description accurate — CN GB 17625 CCC does not substitute for FCC SDoC.

TWO errors: (1) Source URL 'energystar.gov/products/evchargers' returns 404. Corrected to 'energystar.gov/products/ev_chargers' (confirmed live). (2) Version cited as '3.0' is wrong — current ENERGY STAR EVSE specification is Version 1.2 (finalized); Version 2.0 is under development (Draft 1 comment period closed Feb 2025). There is no Version 3.0. All substantive content (efficiency thresholds, voluntary status, rebate/NEVI relevance) is directionally accurate but the specific version number 3.0 must be corrected to 1.2 in all three language fields. mandatory_status 'voluntary' is correct.

OSHA NRTL URL 'osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program' confirmed live and official. NRTL regulatory basis is 29 CFR 1910.7 (row cites 1910.303 — this is the general electrical safety standard that references NRTL; 1910.7 is the NRTL definition regulation). UL 2594 (AC Level 1/2 EVSE) and UL 2202 (DC EVSE) both confirmed as current standards. AHJ permit requirement accurate. Gap description accurate. Minor: row cites 29 CFR 1910.303 as the NRTL recognition authority but the precise NRTL definition is at 29 CFR 1910.7 — both references are standard in practice.

Source URL 'fhwa.dot.gov/environment/alternative_fuel_corridors/resources/nevi_program.cfm' returned 404. Corrected to 'fhwa.dot.gov/environment/nevi/' (confirmed live FHWA NEVI page). Standards and legal citations (IIJA Pub. L. 117-58, 23 CFR Part 680) confirmed. OCPP 2.0.1, open payment, 97% uptime, and 4-port/≥150kW requirements are per 23 CFR Part 680 original rule (Feb 2023). Buy America status is MORE RESTRICTIVE than row implies as of 2026: Trump administration proposed 100% domestic content requirement (Federal Register Feb 12 2026, comment period closed Mar 16 2026); existing waiver being tightened to require US final assembly from Oct 2025 and ≥55% US components from Oct 2026. Row says 'no blanket waiver exists' which is accurate but understates the tightening trend. NEVI guidance was superseded by FHWA Interim Final Guidance Aug 11 2025 — verify all specific numbers against current 23 CFR Part 680 and Aug 2025 guidance.

Source URL 'fhwa.dot.gov/environment/alternative_fuel_corridors/resources/nevi_program.cfm' returned 404. Corrected to 'fhwa.dot.gov/environment/nevi/'. OCPP de facto mandatory claim confirmed — major US networks require OCPP 1.6 or 2.0.1. IEEE 1547-2018 reference for large DCFC interconnection is accurate. State programme details (CEC CALeVIP, CPUC) flagged [UNVERIFIED] in row — appropriate. Gap description accurate. mandatory_status wording ('de facto mandatory for US commercial network participation') is correct.

NFPA URL 'nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-70-standard-development/70' confirmed live. NEC 2023 Article 625 confirmed as governing EVSE installation. NEC 625.54 confirmed as GFCI protection section for cord-connected EVSE (plug-in). NEC 625.22 confirmed as the personnel protection system (listed device) requirement. Row correctly cites both sections. UL 2594 and UL 2231 as referenced standards are accurate. AHJ permit requirement accurate. Gap description accurate.

Same NFPA source URL confirmed live. NEC Article 625.5 listed-equipment requirement confirmed (row also references 625.2 — both exist and are accurate; 625.5 is the listed-equipment mandate, 625.2 is definitions). UL 2594 (Level 1/2) and UL 2202 (DC fast charge) confirmed as correct applicable standards. NRTL listing as AHJ permit prerequisite is accurate. Gap description accurate. Note: row cites 29 CFR 1910.7 correctly as NRTL definition regulation.

Source URL 'ul.com/services/ul-2594-electric-vehicle-supply-equipment' returned 404. Corrected to 'standardscatalog.ul.com/standards/en/standard_2594_2' (UL official standards catalog). UL 2594 Edition 3 (Dec 2022) confirmed current. Standard covers Level 1 and Level 2 EVSE up to 1000 V AC. Mandatory status accurate. Gap description accurate — CCC does not substitute for NRTL. 29 CFR 1910.7 citation correct.

Source URL 'ul.com/services/ul-2202-electric-vehicle-ev-charging-system-equipment' returned 404. Corrected to 'standardscatalog.ul.com/standards/en/standard_2202_2' (UL official standards catalog). UL 2202 Edition 3 (Dec 2022) confirmed current — trinational standard (US/Canada/Mexico). Standard covers DC output chargers. Mandatory status accurate. Gap description accurate. Note: row mentions 'CHAdeMO' in scope — this is technically correct for historical UL 2202 scope but CHAdeMO is being phased out in North America; not an error but could be noted.

Source URL 'shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?UniqueKey=38770' resolves but to ULC 537 (Canadian fire alarm verification standard) — completely wrong product. URL has been corrected to 'standardscatalog.ul.com/standards/en/standard_2231-1' (UL 2231-1, confirmed). Note: UL 2231 is actually a two-part standard (UL 2231-1: General Requirements; UL 2231-2: Particular Requirements for Protection Devices) — the row refers to it as a single standard 'UL 2231' which is acceptable shorthand but could mislead. NEC 625.22 personnel protection system requirement confirmed accurate. Mandatory status accurate. Gap description accurate.
